summary: Term newborns delivered via assisted vaginal methods such as forceps or vacuum have a higher risk for developing TTN because these procedures can cause temporary breathing difficulties post-delivery. The condition typically affects full-term babies who experience some form of mechanical intervention at birth leading to delayed lung fluid clearance resulting in increased respiration rate shortly after birth.. To be precise it involves scenarios involving use of instruments like forcep/vacuum extractors while assisting with deliveries hence options 'a' & 'b'.
